metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace every occurrence of `fsck --fix` in the plan (Why, What, and AC2) with the actual flag `fsck --repair`, verified against `tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'fsck --fix' as one of the three named entry points that 'reconcile unconditionally', but rebar's actual fsck flag is '--repair' (not '--fix'). The golden CLI output files confirm the flag is '--repair [--dry-run] [--limit=N]'; '--fix' does not exist. The doc will teach future agents an incorrect invocation.
findings.location: Why section ('the marker gates the hint, never the repair'); What section ('fsck --fix'); AC2 ('Documents the entry points…')
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'fsck --fix' → 'fsck --repair' throughout the plan; the flag runs the A3 remediation path and is the correct entry point to document.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `fsck --fix` with `fsck --repair` throughout the plan and the resulting `docs/migrations.md`, matching the actual CLI flag in `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`.
findings.finding: The plan names `fsck --fix` as one of the entry points that 'reconcile unconditionally', but the actual CLI flag in the codebase is `fsck --repair` (not `--fix`). Propagating the wrong flag name into `docs/migrations.md` will mislead future agents who try to invoke it.
findings.location: What section: 'the entry points (init/re-init, `fsck --fix`, server startup — all reconcile unconditionally)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'fsck --fix' to 'fsck --repair' in the What section and in AC2, and confirm this is the correct flag in the final doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC covering the link addition (e.g., 'A link to `docs/migrations.md` is present in `CLAUDE.md` or the docs index, verifiable with `grep -q migrations CLAUDE.md`').
findings.finding: The 'link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md' deliverable mentioned in Scope is absent from the Acceptance Criteria, so it has no corresponding observable criterion that would confirm it was done.
findings.location: Scope section ('In: `docs/migrations.md` + a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md') vs. Acceptance Criteria (no bullet covers the link)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fifth AC: '- [ ] A link to `docs/migrations.md` is present in `CLAUDE.md` or the docs index (verifiable with `grep -q migrations CLAUDE.md`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the link to `docs/migrations.md` exists in the docs index and/or `CLAUDE.md`, matching the Scope deliverable.
findings.finding: The Scope section names 'a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md' as an in-scope deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this link was actually added.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fifth AC: '- [ ] A link to `docs/migrations.md` is present in the docs index (and/or `CLAUDE.md`), making the guide discoverable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that `docs/migrations.md` is linked from the docs index and/or CLAUDE.md (e.g., 'A link to `docs/migrations.md` appears in the docs index or CLAUDE.md under a migrations/ensure-pattern heading').
findings.finding: The Scope section states 'a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md' must be added, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this linkage was actually done. Without an AC, the link will not be checked during review and the doc may remain undiscoverable.
findings.location: Scope ('Out: code (WS1-3), tests (WS5)') and AC list (all four AC lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Append a fifth AC: `- [ ] A navigable link to \`docs/migrations.md\` is present in the docs index (e.g., \`docs/README.md\`) and/or \`CLAUDE.md\` so future sessions can discover the pattern.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the cross-reference link in `CLAUDE.md` (or the docs index) pointing to `docs/migrations.md` exists, to make the discoverability deliverable end-state-observable.
findings.finding: A non-deferred deliverable — 'a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md' — is named in both 'What' and 'Scope' but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion, so there is no end-state-observable proof it was completed.
findings.location: Scope section ('In: docs/migrations.md + a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md') and Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs cover only docs/migrations.md content)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`CLAUDE.md` (or the docs index) contains a link to `docs/migrations.md` so future agents can discover the ensure pattern guide.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., `grep -q 'marker-gates-hint-never-repair' docs/migrations.md`, `grep -q 'gc-config' docs/migrations.md`, `grep -q 'A-tier' docs/migrations.md`, `test -f docs/migrations.md`) so done-ness is mechanically verifiable.
findings.finding: No proving command is provided for any of the four ACs; a documentation plan still needs at least a structural check (e.g., grep for required section headings or key terms) to produce objective evidence of completeness.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section with commands such as:
```
test -f docs/migrations.md
grep -q 'gc-config' docs/migrations.md
grep -q 'marker-gates-hint-never-repair' docs/migrations.md
grep -q 'applied-set' docs/migrations.md
grep -q 'A-tier' docs/migrations.md
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note in the plan (and the resulting doc) that `ensures.py` is the WS1-3 artifact and that `docs/migrations.md` is written against its WS1-3 spec; consider adding an AC requiring the doc to be validated against the actual `ensures.py` once WS1-3 land.
findings.finding: `ensures.py` (the registry the doc describes) does not exist in the codebase — it is a WS1-3 artifact the plan correctly marks as out-of-scope. However, the plan does not note this forward-reference dependency, so a reader of the finished `docs/migrations.md` has no way to find the actual registry to consult or verify the doc's accuracy against the code.
findings.location: What section: 'append to the `ensures.py` registry with a stable id + a check-then-act callable'; Scope: 'Out: code (WS1-3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly whether the link should appear in `CLAUDE.md`, in a docs index file, or in both (e.g., 'add a link in `CLAUDE.md` under the Architecture section').
findings.finding: 'docs index/CLAUDE.md' uses a slash that leaves ambiguous whether the link must appear in the docs index, in CLAUDE.md, or in both — a defaultable gap the executor would have to resolve on their own.
findings.location: Scope section: 'a link from the docs index/CLAUDE.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'docs index/CLAUDE.md' with 'CLAUDE.md (and the docs index if one exists)' or pick one target definitively.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether '24h' is a settled design decision for the write-path hint rate (and if so, cite the WS1-3 spec or ADR that fixes it), or replace the specific value with a placeholder like `<rate>` / 'configurable rate' so the doc remains correct regardless of what WS1-3 implements.
findings.finding: The plan specifies '24h-rate-limited write-path hint' as a concrete fact to document, but no 24h rate exists anywhere in the current codebase for this mechanism — the only write-path throttle present uses a 60-second window (reads.py, `if marker_age < 60`), and '24h' in the codebase belongs to the reconciler alert-store dedup window, which is unrelated. `ensures.py` itself does not yet exist (it is WS1–3 code). Documenting a specific parameter value ('24h') that belongs to an as-yet-unwritten implementation decision risks the guide being factually wrong on delivery if WS1–3 chooses a different rate — and since the doc is the discovery artifact, an incorrect parameter silently misinforms every future implementer.
findings.location: What section: '…the applied-set marker + the 24h-rate-limited write-path hint…'; AC line: 'Documents the entry points, the applied-set marker, the rate-limited write-path hint, and the marker-gates-hint-never-repair invariant'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What section and AC2, replace '24h-rate-limited' with 'rate-limited (rate TBD by WS1-3)' or add a parenthetical '(24h — confirm against WS1-3 implementation)'. This makes the doc's dependency on a WS1-3 decision explicit and prevents the guide from asserting a specific value that may not match the code.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or subsection requirement) that migrations.md includes a brief 'why not a standard migration framework' rationale explaining the tradeoff between the custom ensure-registry and established ordered-migration tools (e.g. Alembic/Flyway), so future sessions understand the design decision and don't re-litigate it.
findings.finding: The documentation plan introduces a custom ensure-registry pattern (set-based applied-set marker, check-then-act callable, rate-limited write-path hint, marker-gates-hint-never-repair) that is architecturally novel relative to well-established migration frameworks (Alembic, Django migrations, Flyway/Liquibase) and convergent-apply tools (Ansible/Chef idempotency). The plan's AC for migrations.md does not require the document to explain why this custom pattern was chosen over existing tools, nor to record the tradeoffs against the established alternatives. Future sessions discovering migrations.md will learn the mechanics but not the 'why not Alembic/Django/Flyway' rationale — undermining the document's stated purpose of helping the next agent 'add an ensure correctly and understand the invariants.'
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria — all four ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC or 'What': 'Includes a brief design-rationale note explaining why a custom idempotent ensure-registry (set-based, unconditional reconcile) is used instead of an ordered migration framework (e.g. Alembic/Flyway), and what would need to change to adopt the A-tier committed-version approach.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
